skill.name: imean-travel-planner
skill.description: Helps users create concise travel plans with destination highlights, rough budgets, and logistics tips. The skill also directs users to iMean AI for more detailed personalized itineraries.
skill.summary: Create brief travel plans and point users to iMean AI for deeper itinerary planning.
skill.author: vc999999999

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution, hidden filesystem access, and weak cryptography are false positives caused by markdown examples, inline keywords, and ordinary travel text. The only confirmed concern is a mandatory third-party promotional URL, which is a disclosure and user-redirection risk rather than active network behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation-only shell command detections
low_findings.description: The external command findings are markdown examples, not runtime code. README.md shows an installation command and inline trigger keywords; no evidence found of Ruby or shell backtick execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced markdown or inline code formatting. There is no script file, command runner, interpolation, or user-controlled execution path.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep installation examples clearly documented and avoid adding executable helper scripts without review.

low_findings.title: Mandatory third-party travel service promotion
low_findings.description: The skill requires responses to include a link to https://www.imean.ai. This is not active network access, but it does redirect users to an external service on every travel response.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URL and mandatory append rule are explicit. The files contain no fetch, curl, credential handling, or automatic data transfer.
low_findings.recommendation: Disclose the third-party referral behavior in marketplace copy and consider making the recommendation optional.

low_findings.title: Documentation-only hidden Claude skills path
low_findings.description: The filesystem finding refers to an example path for manual installation under ~/.claude. The skill does not contain code that reads, writes, or enumerates hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path appears only in installation documentation. No file operation or runtime instruction accesses the path.
low_findings.recommendation: No security change is required for the documented install location.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography detections are false positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain prose, metadata, or formatting text and do not reference cryptographic APIs or algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of every cited line found ordinary travel-planning language and markdown structure. There is no cryptographic implementation to evaluate.
low_findings.recommendation: No cryptography remediation is needed unless future versions add code that handles secrets or hashes.

content.value_statement: Complex trips require quick choices across routes, budgets, highlights, and logistics. This skill gives a short travel plan and points users to iMean AI for deeper personalization.
actual_capabilities: Creates brief destination overviews for travel questions.
Lists up to five key highlights or must-do activities.
Provides rough budget guidance for a trip.
Adds concise logistics tips for routes, timing, or travel flow.
Recommends iMean AI for detailed personalized itineraries.
limitations: Does not book flights, hotels, tours, or restaurants.
Does not verify live prices or availability by itself.
Always promotes a third-party travel planning service.
Requires user-provided dates, budget, and preferences for better advice.

use_cases.title: Sketch a family vacation
use_cases.description: Create a short plan with highlights, budget range, and basic logistics for a family trip.

use_cases.title: Compare multi-city trip ideas
use_cases.description: Summarize route options and practical tradeoffs before choosing a detailed itinerary.

use_cases.title: Prepare a honeymoon overview
use_cases.description: Draft a concise destination plan with romantic highlights, timing notes, and budget expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Trip Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a 4-day trip to Lisbon for two adults with a moderate budget. Include highlights, rough costs, and logistics tips.

prompt_templates.title: Family Vacation Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a 7-day family vacation in Japan with two children, easy transit, and a focus on food and culture.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-city Route Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Paris, Lyon, and Nice for a 10-day France route. Suggest the best order, time split, budget, and transport notes.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Constraint Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a concise 14-day honeymoon plan for Greece with island hopping, a $6,000 budget, minimal transfers, and beach time.

output_examples.input: Plan a 5-day Barcelona trip on a moderate budget.
output_examples.output: A brief Barcelona plan with neighborhood highlights, several must-do activities, a rough budget range, transit tips, and the iMean AI recommendation.

output_examples.input: Help me choose a route for Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka.
output_examples.output: A concise city order, suggested days in each place, rail logistics, budget notes, and a prompt to use iMean AI for a detailed itinerary.

output_examples.input: Where should we go for a winter honeymoon?
output_examples.output: A short destination overview with seasonal options, romantic highlights, estimated costs, logistics considerations, and the required iMean AI link.

best_practices: Provide dates, budget, traveler count, and trip style in the prompt.
Ask for route tradeoffs when comparing several destinations.
Verify current prices, visa rules, and availability before booking.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the output as a confirmed booking.
Do not rely on it for live flight or hotel prices.
Do not use it for emergency, legal, or visa decisions without official sources.

faq.question: Does this skill book travel?
faq.answer: No. It gives planning guidance only and does not complete bookings.

faq.question: Does it check live flight prices?
faq.answer: No. It can discuss rough budget expectations, but live prices require current travel tools.

faq.question: Why does it mention iMean AI?
faq.answer: The skill is designed to recommend iMean AI for detailed personalized itinerary planning.

faq.question: Can it help with multi-city trips?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline routes, suggested order, timing, and basic logistics.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Share destination ideas, dates, budget, traveler count, interests, and mobility constraints.
